name: set_component_property
description: Set a serialized property value on a component attached to a GameObject. Works with any serialized field visible in the Unity Inspector: booleans, integers, floats, strings, enums, Vector2/3/4, Color, Rect, and LayerMask. For object/component references, use the 'set_component_reference' tool instead. Property names use the serialized field name (e.g. 'm_UseGravity', 'm_IsKinematic', 'm_Mass' for Rigidbody). The tool also tries to match common C# property names automatically (e.g. 'useGravity' will find 'm_UseGravity').

game_object.description: The name of the GameObject that has the component.

component.description: The type name of the component, e.g. 'Rigidbody', 'BoxCollider', 'AudioSource', 'PlayerController'.

property.description: The name of the property to set. Can use the C# property name (e.g. 'useGravity', 'isKinematic', 'mass') or the serialized field name (e.g. 'm_UseGravity'). The tool will try to find the correct match.

value.description: The value to set, as a string. Examples: 'true'/'false' for booleans, '1.5' for floats, '10' for integers, 'Continuous' for enums, '1,2,3' or '(1, 2, 3)' for Vector3.
